hero.badge: Protocol v1 · BSC Mainnet · Whitepaper v3
hero.sub: On-chain MRV, IPFS evidence anchoring, and SES-ready reporting. Every environmental action — hashed, stored, and auditable.

protocol.heading: Verifiable by Design.
protocol.p1: SustainToken is environmental impact infrastructure — not a carbon credit marketplace. We provide the cryptographic primitives and on-chain registry for organizations to record, hash, and anchor sustainability actions with institutional-grade evidence.
protocol.p2: Every action produces a SHA-256 fingerprint of its evidence file, a Content ID pinned to IPFS, and an immutable transaction hash on BSC — making greenwashing detectable and sustainability claims independently verifiable.
protocol.l3Desc: BSC smart contract · TxHash · Immutable log

howItWorks.heading: Seven Steps. One Verifiable Record.
howItWorks.sub: From action to on-chain evidence — a deterministic, auditable trail with no trusted intermediary.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Org records a sustainability action with metadata
howItWorks.step2Desc: Evidence file attached (PDF, CSV, sensor data)
howItWorks.step4Desc: File pinned to IPFS; Content ID returned
howItWorks.step5Desc: Hash + CID written to BSC smart contract
howItWorks.step7Desc: Sustainability Evidence Score ready for reporting

architecture.heading: Protocol Stack — 4 Layers
architecture.sub: A modular infrastructure stack designed for verifiability, interoperability, and institutional-grade auditability.
architecture.layer4Desc: Web Dashboard · Mobile · APIs · Utilities · Corporates · Municipalities
architecture.layer3Desc: Sustain Action Standard (SAS) · MRV Rules & Validation · SES — Environmental Score · Anti-Greenwashing Checks
architecture.layer2Desc: On-chain Registration · Smart Contracts · Audit Trail · On-chain Anchors · BSC Mainnet · Sustain Doc Hub
architecture.layer1Desc: Evidence Capture · Cryptographic Hash (SHA-256) · Decentralized Storage (IPFS CID) · No PII in public layer
architecture.climateSub: Where Sustain sits in the global climate infrastructure — the data and verification layer between accounting standards and markets.
architecture.climateAppsItems: ESG Platforms · Corporate Dashboards · City Programs
architecture.sustainLayerAriaLabel: Sustain Protocol — Data and Verification Layer

pilots.heading: Prove Sustainability. Verifiably.
pilots.sub: Run a structured 90-day pilot with on-chain evidence from day one. No greenwashing. No trust required.
pilots.ctaHeading: Run a verifiable sustainability pilot in 90 days.
pilots.ctaSub: Organizations, municipalities, and enterprises. No pre-existing infrastructure required.
pilots.card1Desc: Monitor kWh/day normalized reduction against a verified baseline. Evidence: utility bills, sensor data.
pilots.card2Desc: Document waste diversion rates with certified weighing reports and photographic evidence.
pilots.card3Desc: Track fuel consumption and compute Scope 1 emissions per km, anchored with fuel receipts.
pilots.energyNote: kWh/day normalization removes billing period bias. Methodology: (baseline_rate − measure_rate) / baseline_rate.
pilots.sha256Tooltip: Cryptographic fingerprint of the evidence file. Any modification changes this hash.
pilots.ipfsCidTooltip: Content-addressed identifier on IPFS. No PII exposed in public layer.
pilots.txHashTooltip: Immutable on-chain record on BSC. Publicly auditable via BscScan.

compliance.heading: Built for Every Framework.
compliance.sub: SustainToken's data structure is designed to map directly to major sustainability reporting frameworks — no extra conversion layer required.
compliance.card1Desc: Scope 1, 2, 3 emission categories mapped to action types.
compliance.card2Desc: Climate-related disclosures aligned with IFRS S1/S2 structure.
compliance.card3Desc: European Sustainability Reporting Standards mapping for EU-regulated entities.
compliance.card4Desc: Greenhouse gas quantification and verification standard compliance.

impact.heading: Real-Time Impact. Preview.
impact.sub: Static in v1 — API-ready for live data in v2. Every metric backed by on-chain records.

community.heading: Open Infrastructure. Real Evidence.
community.sub: Community nodes generate the evidence base that validates the protocol. Participation is non-incentivized and transparent.
community.blockADesc: Public record of community pilot nodes and their verified actions.
community.blockBDesc: Non-incentivized. Open participation. Your actions contribute to the protocol's public evidence base.
community.formNote: No token reward. Non-incentivized participation only.

token.heading: The SustainToken.
token.desc: SustainToken (SUST) is the protocol's utility and governance token. It is not a carbon credit, not a speculative asset, and not a financial instrument. It represents verified environmental action — backed by cryptographic evidence.

governance.heading: Transparent. On-chain. Auditable.
governance.desc: Protocol governance is progressively decentralized. V1 decisions are documented on-chain. V2 will introduce token-weighted proposals.
governance.card1Desc: All protocol decisions, schema updates, and whitepaper versions anchored to BSC.
governance.card2Desc: Full protocol specification available on-chain and via GitHub mirror. IPFS-pinned.
governance.card3Desc: SUST holders will vote on protocol parameters, schema extensions, and pilot standards.

developers.heading: Build on the Protocol.
developers.sub: Open schemas, documented APIs (v2 roadmap), and a public contract on BSC. Integrate verifiable sustainability into your stack.

resources.heading: Protocol Documentation.
resources.card1Desc: Full protocol specification. Environmental Impact Infrastructure for Verifiable Sustainability. IPFS-pinned.
resources.card2Desc: Versión en español de la especificación del protocolo. IPFS-pinned.
resources.card3Desc: Smart contract on BSC Mainnet. Immutable document registry — whitepaper versions, protocol specs, governance anchors.

contact.heading: Ready to build verifiable sustainability?
contact.desc: Whether you're an institution running a pilot, a developer integrating the protocol, or an organization mapping to compliance frameworks — let's talk.
